The '''''Book of Imperial Shuttle Plans: Cygnus Spaceworks''''' is [[Fanon|fan art]] produced under the Lawrence Miller Design label, apparently in [[1984]]. Consisting of blueprints for what later official works would identify as the [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class T-4a shuttle]], it has sometimes been mistaken for a [[Lucasfilm]]-authorized product. Certain elements described in the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity, as well as the [[canon]]ical [[Cygnus Spaceworks]], derive from these blueprints. The blueprints also contain a list of [[Star system/Legends|star systems]], many of which share names with real stars visible from Earth or locations from other science fiction franchises, such as [[MemoryAlpha:Star Trek|''Star Trek'']] and [[W:c:Tardis:Doctor Who|''Doctor Who'']].

==Contents==
The "book" consists of an envelope with three blueprint sheets for an "Imperial Shuttle" that is essentially the [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class T-4a shuttle]] from [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']]. It apparently uses the name of [[Cygnus Spaceworks/Legends|Cygnus Spaceworks]] almost a decade before any official source,<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|157|Cygnus Spaceworks}}</ref><ref name="Message to Spacers">{{WizardsCite|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/Mesop7|text=Message to Spacers 7|int=Message to Spacers 7}}</ref><ref name="Databank Theta">{{DB|starship|thetaclassshuttle|Imperial ''Theta''-class shuttle}}</ref><ref name="RECG">''[[Rebellion Era Campaign Guide]]''</ref><ref name="Written Word">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/source/writtenword/index.html?page=1|text=The Written Word|int=The Written Word}}</ref><ref name="Written Word Endnotes">{{Blog|JSarek|8|Endnotes for The Written Word}} confirms the connection between the canonical Cygnus Spaceworks and this fan publication.</ref> and it has been discussed alongside official publications by at least one influential fan site, the ''[[Curtis Saxton|Star Wars Technical Commentaries]]''.<ref name="SWTC">{{WebCite|url=http://www.theforce.net/swtc/lambda.html|text=Star Wars Technical Commentaries: ''Lambda''-class shuttles|archivedate=20220131220745}}</ref> It is not clear how the name of Cygnus Spaceworks could have found its way into official material from here, but it appears that [[Lora Johnson]], one of the first official authors to reference Cygnus Spaceworks, worked with Miller on at least one project (a ''Star Trek'' fan production entitled the ''U.S.S. Enterprise Officer's Manual - Revised'').<ref name="LCARS">{{WebCite|url=http://www.cygnus-x1.net/links/lcars/TCH.php|text=''U.S.S. Enterprise Officer's Manual - Revised'' listing at The Star Trek LCARS Blueprints Database|archivedate=20220325222230}}</ref>

Beyond the actual shuttle plan (which closely resembles the published set plan in [[1983]]'s ''[[The Art of Star Wars Episode VI: Return of the Jedi]]''), the only element which bears any direct connection with [[canon]] is the name "[[General]] A.F. Motti". While no such character is in official sources, a character in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']] is [[Admiral]] [[Conan Antonio Motti]].

The shuttle is said to be used in the Cygnus Star Empire, rather than the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Similarly, the shuttle is described as meeting construction codes in a list of star systems which includes real stars visible from [[Wikipedia:Earth|Earth]] and fictional locations from other science fiction series such as [[W:c:Tardis:Doctor Who|''Doctor Who'']] and the original [[MemoryAlpha:Star Trek: The Original Series|''Star Trek'']] series. If taken literally, this list implies that the shuttle satisfies the safety standards of the [[W:c:Tardis:Dalek|Daleks]], the [[MemoryAlpha:Romulan|Romulans]], and a society based on [[MemoryAlpha:Chicago Mobs of the Twenties|Chicago gangsters of the 1920s]].

==Appearances==
{{App
|characters=
|organisms=
|droids=
|events=
|locations=*'''Altair'''—this is a [[Wikipedia:Altair|real star]], but the system has been mentioned [[MemoryAlpha:Altair system|several times]] in ''Star Trek''. It is unlikely that there is a direct connection between the blueprints and the Legends [[Altair 9]].
*'''Atrios'''—a [http://www.geos.tv/index.php/episode/who/103 fictional planet] from ''Doctor Who''.
*'''Beta 6'''—the "Beta VI colony" is mentioned in the classic ''Star Trek'' episode "[[MemoryAlpha:The Squire of Gothos|The Squire of Gothos]]."
*'''Ceti Alpha'''—although resembling the name of the real star [[Wikipedia:Alpha_Ceti|Alpha Ceti]], the specific name of [[MemoryAlpha:Ceti Alpha system|Ceti Alpha]] is from ''Star Trek''.
*'''Coridan'''—a [[MemoryAlpha:Coridan (planet)|fictitious planet]] from ''Star Trek''. The ''Star Wars'' Legends [[Corridan sector/Legends|Corridan sector]] may be a reference to the original location in ''Star Trek''.
*'''Cygnus'''—the putative capital of the [[Cygnus Star Empire]], giving rise to the name of the [[Cygnus Spaceworks/Legends|Cygnus Spaceworks]] that apparently found its way from here into canon. The Cygnus Star System is the only location that seems genuinely original to the blueprint; it is probably named for the real constellation [[Wikipedia:Cygnus|Cygnus]], the Swan, although the name of the Pyxidis Asteroid Belt, said to be in this star system, would rather imply a location in the constellation [[Wikipedia:Pyxis|Pyxis]], the Compass. However, planets with similar names are also known in ''Star Wars'' ([[Cygnus B system|Cygnus B]]), ''Star Trek'' ([[MemoryAlpha:Alpha Cygnus IX|Alpha Cygnus IX]]), ''Doctor Who'' ([http://www.bbc.co.uk/doctorwho/classic/episodeguide/nightmareofeden/continuity.shtml Cygnus Gap] and [http://www.bbc.co.uk/doctorwho/classic/episodeguide/planetofevil/continuity.shtml Cygnus A]), and the British sci-fi series ''Blake's 7'' ([http://www.hillcity-comics.com/blake_7/eps3.htm Cygnus Alpha]). ''Cygnus Alpha'' was also apparently the name of a [http://www.who-central.co.uk/InterviewCornell.html sci-fi fanzine]. Any one of these could have inspired the name used here.
*'''Daria'''—a [http://www.johnkennethmuir.com/ConcordanceDG.html fictional planet] from [[Wikipedia:Space: 1999|''Space: 1999'']].
*'''Delta Magna'''—a [http://www.mentalis.force9.co.uk/DWID/TB/Stories/5E.htm fictitious planet] from ''Doctor Who''.
*'''Deneb'''—a [[Wikipedia:Deneb|real star]] also [[MemoryAlpha:Deneb|mentioned]] in ''Star Trek''.
*'''Holberg 917'''—[[MemoryAlpha:Holberg 917G|Holberg 917G]] is a fictional planet in ''Star Trek''.
*'''Janus'''—[[MemoryAlpha:Janus VI|Janus VI]] is a fictional planet in ''Star Trek''.
*'''Omega'''—in ''Star Trek'', Holberg 917G (see above) is in the [[MemoryAlpha:Omega system|Omega system]], and there is also a planet named [[MemoryAlpha:Omega IV|Omega IV]].
*'''Piri'''—a [http://www.johnkennethmuir.com/ConcordanceNS.html fictional planet] from ''Space: 1999''.
*'''Pollux'''—a [[Wikipedia:Pollux_(star)|real star]], also the primary for the fictional planet [[MemoryAlpha:Pollux IV|Pollux IV]] in ''Star Trek''.
*'''Remus'''—a [[MemoryAlpha:Remus|fictional planet]] in ''Star Trek''.
*'''Rigel'''—a [[Wikipedia:Rigel|real star]] and a [[MemoryAlpha:Rigel|location]] in ''Star Trek''. It may be only a coincidence that a planet named [[Rigel VII]] had appeared in official ''Star Wars'' material in 1978.
*'''Sigma Iotia'''—[[MemoryAlpha:Sigma Iotia II|Sigma Iotia II]] is a fictional planet from ''Star Trek''.
*'''Skaro'''—the fictional [[W:c:Tardis:Skaro|homeworld]] of the [[W:c:tardis:Dalek|Daleks]] in ''Doctor Who''.
*'''Sol'''—the Latin name for the [[Wikipedia:Sun|Sun]]. It is popular as a designation in sci-fi series such as [[MemoryAlpha:Sol|''Star Trek'']].
*'''Talos'''—the name of a [[MemoryAlpha:Talos|star system]] in ''Star Trek''.
*'''Tau Ceti'''—a [[Wikipedia:Tau_Ceti|real star]] that has also appeared [[MemoryAlpha:Tau Ceti system|several times]] in ''Star Trek''.
*'''Vega'''—[[Wikipedia:Vega|real star]] and also the name of a [[MemoryAlpha:Vega|star system]] in ''Star Trek''.
*'''Xerius'''—reference unknown, though it is the name of a [[MemoryAlpha:Xerius|Romulan]] character in the 1970s [[MemoryAlpha:Star Trek: The Animated Series|''Star Trek'' animated series]].
*'''Zeon'''—a [[MemoryAlpha:Zeon %28planet%29|fictional planet]] in ''Star Trek''.
